---
title: "Field Validation Settings"
---

You can use preconfigured validators such as length limits, required fields, and regular expressions pattern matching, required value in the configuration UI as well as leverage a customizable script-based validation using a process model.

The field validation settings determine if a field value is valid or not. Invalid fields prevent submission of document review user tasks giving you strict control over what needs review, and what can move forward in the process. In IDP configuration, validation rules are saved along with the associated field definition.

If any of the field validation criteria fail during processing, the field is flagged for manual review.

To configure field validation settings,

1.  In the Edit Field pane, under the Settings tab, in the Field Validation box, click Add. The field validation menu is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-  Field length: Select this option to set the minimum and maximum number of characters allowed in this field.
-  Regular Expression: Select this option to ensure that the field’s value matches the pattern defined by this regular expression. For example, an email address follows a typical pattern.
-  Required Field: Select this option to indicate that this field is mandatory.
-  Existing Process: Select this option to open an existing process modeling template for validating fields.
-  Create Process: Select this option to create a template in process modeling to validate field values. For more information, see
2.  Click Save. The field validation settings are saved.  Note: Preconfigured validation settings are located on the Settings tab for header fields and reasoning fields, and located in the Add Column dialog box for tables. Process-based validation settings are found on the Settings tab for header, reasoning, and table fields.
